value.Title: Restaurant Technologies Reports Some 400 Million Lbs of UCO Recycled in 2025

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGP-6Y93-S0VT-G1GR-00000-00Restaurant Technologies Reports Some 400 Million Lbs of UCO Recycled in 20252026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:13ZLexisNexisRestaurant Technologies Reports Some 400 Million Lbs of UCO Recycled in 2025

Restaurant Technologies, Inc. (RTI) reported it delivered almost 720 million pounds of fresh cooking oil and exceeded its own record by recycling over 393 million pounds of used cooking oil (UCO) in 2025.

With the support of renewable energy partners such as Phillips 66, the company said it converted nearly 100 percent of its UCO into renewable diesel or biodiesel for vehicles on the road, or into sustainable aviation fuel foraircraft.

"I am tremendously proud of our organization, our sustainability accomplishments, and our continued efforts to build a cleaner, healthier planet," said Diana Geseking, Chief Legal Officer and ESG Committee chair. "I look forward to expanding our goals to deliver measurable environmental impact with our customers and renewable energy partners."

As RTI continues to grow, the impact can be felt around the country in the food we eat, with customers that include McDonald's, Shake Shack, Sheetz, Kwik Trip, Kroger, and over 45,000 foodservice operators nationwide.

In 2025, the company helped avoid over 20 million plastic jugs by delivering fresh oil in bulk. By removing all packaging waste from the oil delivery process, RTI saves about 31.5 million pounds of trash, 9.5 million cubic feet of landfill space, and estimates greenhouse gas (GHG CO2e) emissions reductions at over 85 million pounds. This is equivalent to saving about 5,202 homes' energy use for one year.

"Prioritizing our corporate impact as an industry leader reflects the values thathelpguide our organization," said Jeff Kiesel,President and CEO of Restaurant Technologies. "As we grow and continue to elevate kitchens nationwide, we remain committed to delivering meaningful environmental impact for our customers and communities."

The environmental impact goes beyond reducing waste in the kitchen. By recycling oil that was first used to feed our communities into biofuel, RTI and its renewable energy partners are helping to conserve farmland from being used exclusively for oil crops. Additionally, any resulting renewable diesel that replaces traditional petroleum-based fuels can cut tailpipe emissions by up to 75 percent. RTI, its customers, and renewable energy partners continue to help define sustainability across the industry and support a cleaner, healthier world for all.

Restaurant Technologies is a provider of automated commercial kitchen solutions for more than45,000 customers nationwide. Headquartered in Mendota Heights, Minnesota, Restaurant Technologies is privately held and operates 41depots nationwide with more than 1,200 employees.

More information and complete details:

www.rti-inc.com

((Comments on this story may be sent to newsdesk@closeupmedia.com))

value.Title: Seminole Financial Services Finances Two Solar Projects in Minnesota

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JKH-1BD3-RW07-P2G6-00000-00Seminole Financial Services Finances Two Solar Projects in Minnesota2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:27ZLexisNexisSeminole Financial Services Finances Two Solar Projects in Minnesota

Key Highlights:

* Seminole has committed over $3.5 billion in renewable energy financing since 2009.

* Seminole has financed over 400 solar and wind installations across the US and Hawaii.

* Seminole's renewable energy finance programs have supported 2.0+ GW in installed capacity.

Original Press Release:

Belleair Bluffs, Florida, May 1 -- Seminole Financial Services issued the following news release:

Seminole Financial Services ("SFS"), a leading provider of debt and tax credit equity financing for renewable energy transactions, announced the closing of two Solar transactions totaling $2.5 million perm loan. These solar projects located in Minnesota have a capacity of 2.69 MW. Since 2009, Seminole has committed over $3.5 billion in construction and permanent debt financing plus tax credit equity for more than 400 solar and wind installations throughout the continental U.S. and Hawaii, for the equivalent of 2.0+ GW in installed capacity. For further details about Seminole Financial Services' renewable energy finance programs, please contact Chris Diaz, Principal, at (727) 460-0578.

About

Seminole Financial Services was founded with the goal of financing Renewable Energy and Real Estate projects. For over a decade, we've provided superior returns to our investors by creating innovative capital solutions for our development partners. Our vertically integrated team takes a collaborative approach to working with our stakeholders so that, together, we can build a more sustainable future. To date, we have directly funded over $3.5 Billion in capital for Renewable Energy and Multi-Family Real Estate projects nationwide.

Seminole's consistent returns and relationship-focus are grounded in the investment principles of its founder, Bob Banks. For over 30 years, Mr. Banks powered the success of the Midland Companies. During his tenure and title of Owner and CEO, Mr. Banks closed over 2,500 real estate investments and consistently generated above-market returns.

In 1999, Mr. Banks successfully sold the Midland Companies to a larger public entity and retired in 2003. Following his retirement, the funds' performance began to suffer and, in 2008 the pension funds asked Mr. Banks if he would consider exiting retirement to resume fund management utilizing his relationship-focused approach.

[Category: Renewable Energy]

Source: Seminole Financial Services

value.Title: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission : Northern States Power Company , a Minnesota corporation submits tariff filing per 35.13(a)(2)(iii: 2026-05-05 Plum Creek E&P - 793 - 0.0.0 to be effective 4/28/2026 under ER26-2461 Filing Type : 10

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-BKC3-S0RF-C55B-00000-00Federal Energy Regulatory Commission: Northern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporation submits tariff filing per 35.13(a)(2)(iii: 2026-05-05 Plum Creek E&P - 793 - 0.0.0 to be effective 4/28/2026 under ER26-2461 Filing Type : 102026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:35ZLexisNexisFederal Energy Regulatory Commission: Northern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporation submits tariff filing per 35.13(a)(2)(iii: 2026-05-05 Plum Creek E&P - 793 - 0.0.0 to be effective 4/28/2026 under ER26-2461 Filing Type : 10

Washington: Northern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporation has submitted the following document to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission:

The Honorable Debbie-Anne A. ReeseSecretaryFederal Energy Regulatory Commission888 First Street, NEWashington, DC 20426RE: Northern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporationRevised Engineering & Procurement Agreement for MISO Project J2104Service Agreement No. 793-NSPDocket No. ER26-____-000Dear Secretary Reese:Pursuant to Section 205 of the Federal Power Act, 16 U.S.C § 824d (2018), and Section 35.13 of the regulations of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (“Commission”), 18 C.F.R § 35. 13 (2026), Northern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporation (“NSPM”), hereby submits for filing the Revised Engineering and Procurement Agreement for MISO Project J2104 (“Revised Agreement”) between NSPM, Geronimo Power, LLC (“Geronimo”), and Midcontinent Independent System Operator, Inc. (“MISO”). Individually, NSPM, Geronimo, and MISO are referred to herein as “Party” and collectively as the “Parties”. The Agreement is submitted in accordance with Order No. 7141 and the Commission ’ s eTariff filing requirements.1. BackgroundNSPM and its utility operating company affiliate Northern States Power Company, a Wisconsin corporation (together the “NSP Companies”) are wholly owned utility operating company subsidiaries of Xcel Energy Inc., and are engaged in the business of generating, transmitting, distributing and selling electric power and energy and related services in the States of Minnesota, Michigan, North Dakota, South Dakota, and Wisconsin. The NSP Companies are transmission-owning members of the Midcontinent Independent System Operator, Inc. (“MISO”) and provide most transmission service pursuant to the MISO Open Access Transmission, Energy and Operating Reserve Markets Tariff on file with and accepted by the Commission. However, certain services are, or have been, provided pursuant to individual grandfathered agreements or the Xcel Energy Operating Companies Open Access Transmission Tariff (“Xcel Energy OATT”) on file with and accepted by the Commission.2 Individual NSPM service agreements are on file with the Commission in NSPM ’ s FERC Electric Tariff Second Revised Volume No. 3.1 Electronic Tariff Filings, Order No. 714, 124 FERC ¶ 61,270 (2008); order on clarification, Order No. 714-A, 147 FERC ¶ 61,115 (2014).2 The Xcel Energy OATT was restated in eTariff and accepted for filing in Public Service Company of Colorado, Docket No. ER16-1422-000, unpublished letter order (August 16, 2016).The Honorable Debbie-Anne A. ReeseMay 5, 2026Page 2 of 6Geronimo is a wholly owned subsidiary of National Grid Renewables, a leading renewable energy company with a nationwide development portfolio. Under National Grid Renewables ’ ownership, Geronimo has continued its core focus on project origination and early-stage development while leveraging broader construction, financing, and asset management capabilities.On April 28, 2026, the Parties executed the Revised Agreement modifying certain provisions of the Engineering and Procurement Agreement for MISO Project J2104 (Original Agreement). The Original Agreement was executed as a conforming agreement under Attachment W-1 of the Xcel Energy OATT, effective June 20, 2025.2. The Instant FilingThe modifications to the Original Agreement proposed herein reflect necessary changes that occurred following its execution, including an updated in-service date, an extension of the termination provision in absence of a Generator Interconnection Agreement, and an increase to the security amount associated with upgrades required to accommodate MISO Project J2104. In addition, the Revised Agreement includes minor administrative modifications that may be considered non-conforming. Accordingly, the Revised Agreement is being filed out of an abundance of caution.Section 14 and Appendix A identify the minor modifications that NSPM deems to be non-conforming. Consistent with filing requirements, NSPM submits the Revised Agreement in both redline and clean formats.3. Proposed Effective Date and Request for Waiver of NoticeNSPM respectfully requests that the Commission grant waiver of its sixty (60) day prior notice requirement3 and allow the Revised Agreement to become effective on April 28, 2026, the date of execution. NSPM submits that good cause exists to grant the requested waiver. It is the Commission ’ s policy to allow waiver of the prior notice requirements for service agreements under a tariff filed within thirty (30) days of the requested effective date.4 Here, the Revised Agreement is being filed within 7 days of its requested effective date of April 28, 2026. Moreover, the Parties support an April 28, 2026, effective date for the Revised Agreement. NSPM requests April 28, 2026, to provide certainty to the Parties as to the status of the Revised Agreement.4. Description of Documents Being Submitted with Filing5a. This transmittal letter,b. A clean version of Service Agreement No. 793-NSP, andc. A redline version of Service Agreement No. 793-NSP.3 18 C.F.R § 35.3(a) (2026).4 Prior Notice and Filing Requirements under Part II of the Federal Power Act, 64 FERC ¶ 61,139 at 61,984 (1993). See also PJM Interconnection, LLC, 194 FERC ¶ 61,096 at P 14 (2026).5 The Original Agreement was reported on the EQR as 778-NSP. This eTariff filing designates the Revised Agreement as 793-NSP.The Honorable Debbie-Anne A. ReeseMay 5, 2026Page 3 of 65. Communications and CorrespondenceCommunications and correspondence with respect to this filing should be directed to each of the following: 6Service and PostingAn electronic version or notice of this filing will be served upon each of the state commissions having jurisdiction over NSPM and on representatives of the Parties. A courtesy copy will be provided to the Division of Electric Power Regulation (Central). In addition, a copy of this filing will be posted at the offices of NSPM at 414 Nicollet Mall in Minneapolis, Minnesota.Please direct any questions regarding this filing to Elizabeth Walkup at (612) 216-8223.Respectfully submitted,/s/ Jonathan EserJonathan EserAssistant General Counsel & Senior Director, Transmission Business RelationsXcel Energy Services Inc.On behalf ofNorthern States Power Company, a Minnesota corporation6 NSPM requests waiver of Section 385.203(b)(3) of the Commission ’ s regulations to permit the designation of more than two persons upon whom service is to be made in this proceeding. 18 C.F.R § 385.203(b)(3) (2026).Jonathan EserAssistant General Counsel & Senior Director, Transmission Business RelationsXcel Energy Services Inc.701 Pennsylvania Ave. NW Suite 250Washington, DC 20004Email: Jonathan.Eser@xcelenergy.comMichèle B. value.Title: American Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-99B3-RRXR-P0N2-00000-00American Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisAmerican Securities Completes Sale Of CPM To Rosebank Industries

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- American Securities LLC ("American Securities"), a leading U.S. private equity firm, today announced the completion of the sale of CPM Holdings, Inc. ("CPM"), a global leader in highly engineered process equipment and automation solutions, to Rosebank Industries plc (LON: ROSE) ("Rosebank"), in a transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion.*
Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, CPM is an innovative leader in highly engineered processing equipment used in oilseed, animal feed production, renewable energy, plant-based foods and industrial materials. Since American Securities' investment in 2018, the company has strengthened its leadership position across key end markets while expanding its higher-margin aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings. CPM increased its presence in attractive growth areas, including renewable energy, and invested significantly in talent, technology, and operational capabilities to support long-term growth. The company also continued to drive innovation through initiatives such as Monarc, a proprietary technology designed to help customers produce clean label products more safely and efficiently at scale.


"We are incredibly proud of what the CPM team accomplished during our partnership," said Michael Sand, Partner at American Securities. "Together, we strengthened the company's operational capabilities, expanded into new markets, and advanced innovative technologies that position CPM for continued long-term growth. We thank Dave Webster and the entire CPM team and wish them continued success with Rosebank."
*Excludes potential earn-out payment based on the achievement of certain FY26 performance targets.
About American Securities
Founded in 1994, American Securities is a leading U.S. private equity firm that invests in North American companies, primarily in the industrial and B2B services sectors. With $23 billion under management, we partner with businesses generating $200 million to $2 billion in annual revenues. We combine deep sector expertise, differentiated insights and proven internal capabilities to serve as transformational partners that drive growth and build enduring value. Our investment philosophy emphasizes capital preservation through disciplined investing and hands-on engagement, paired with repeatable value creation processes and operational excellence. American Securities is based in New York with an office in Shanghai. For more information, please visit .
About CPM
Founded in 1883, CPM has evolved from its origins as the California Pellet Mill into a diversified industrial business. Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, the company has become an innovative leader in the design and manufacture of highly engineered process equipment and automation systems. CPM serves a global customer base, operating through three core segments: Industrial Solutions, Engineered Solutions and Process Solutions primarily serving the animal feed, oilseed processing, renewable energy, food, and other industrial markets. The company operates 35 facilities globally and maintains a commercial presence in more than 100 countries, with approximately 1,700 employees.
Press Contact
Prosek Partners
[email protected]
SOURCE American Securities LLC

value.Title: American Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-4143-RV0J-R2SN-00000-00American Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisAmerican Securities Completes Sale of CPM to Rosebank Industries

Key Highlights:

* CPM strengthens leadership in key end markets and expands aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings.

* Company increases presence in renewable energy and invests in talent, technology, and operational capabilities.

* CPM drives innovation through proprietary technology Monarc for clean label product production.

Original Press Release:

May 12 -- American Securities LLC issued the following news release:

American Securities LLC ("American Securities"), a leading U.S. private equity firm, today announced the completion of the sale of CPM Holdings, Inc. ("CPM"), a global leader in highly engineered process equipment and automation solutions, to Rosebank Industries plc (LON: ROSE) ("Rosebank"), in a transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion.[*]

Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, CPM is an innovative leader in highly engineered processing equipment used in oilseed, animal feed production, renewable energy, plant-based foods and industrial materials. Since American Securities' investment in 2018, the company has strengthened its leadership position across key end markets while expanding its higher-margin aftermarket and recurring revenue offerings. CPM increased its presence in attractive growth areas, including renewable energy, and invested significantly in talent, technology, and operational capabilities to support long-term growth. The company also continued to drive innovation through initiatives such as Monarc, a proprietary technology designed to help customers produce clean label products more safely and efficiently at scale.

"We are incredibly proud of what the CPM team accomplished during our partnership," said Michael Sand, Partner at American Securities. "Together, we strengthened the company's operational capabilities, expanded into new markets, and advanced innovative technologies that position CPM for continued long-term growth. We thank Dave Webster and the entire CPM team and wish them continued success with Rosebank."

About American Securities

Founded in 1994, American Securities is a leading U.S. private equity firm that invests in North American companies, primarily in the industrial and B2B services sectors. With $23 billion under management, we partner with businesses generating $200 million to $2 billion in annual revenues. We combine deep sector expertise, differentiated insights and proven internal capabilities to serve as transformational partners that drive growth and build enduring value. Our investment philosophy emphasizes capital preservation through disciplined investing and hands-on engagement, paired with repeatable value creation processes and operational excellence. American Securities is based in New York with an office in Shanghai. For more information, please visit www.american-securities.com.

About CPM

Founded in 1883, CPM has evolved from its origins as the California Pellet Mill into a diversified industrial business. Headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota, the company has become an innovative leader in the design and manufacture of highly engineered process equipment and automation systems. CPM serves a global customer base, operating through three core segments: Industrial Solutions, Engineered Solutions and Process Solutions primarily serving the animal feed, oilseed processing, renewable energy, food, and other industrial markets. The company operates 35 facilities globally and maintains a commercial presence in more than 100 countries, with approximately 1,700 employees.

Footnote:

[*] Excludes potential earn-out payment based on the achievement of certain FY26 performance targets.

[Category: Private Equity and Venture Capital, Investments, Mergers and Acquisitions]

Source: American Securities LLC

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

ALLIANT ENERGY CORPORATION EX-DIVIDENDS SINCE APR 30

The shares decreased by 2.3% in the 6 trading sessions since the ex-dividend date of April 30. The company paid a dividend of 53.50c per share. Based on dividends of $2.08 per share for the trailing twelve months and the last price of $71.76 the dividend yield is 2.9%.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 23, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Alliant Energy Net Profit up 17%

Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $810m, up 17% from $690m in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 17% from $2.69 in FY2024 to $3.15 in FY2025.

Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 671.0 points (2.4%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.3%. The stock last traded at $71.76. There were 2,274,239 shares worth $163.2 million traded today; the volume was 1.1 times the average daily trading volume of 2.1 million shares.

SECTION 4 CORPORATE PROFILE

4.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

ALLIANT ENERGY CORPORATION EX-DIVIDENDS SINCE APR 30

The shares decreased by 2.3% in the 6 trading sessions since the ex-dividend date of April 30. The company paid a dividend of 53.50c per share. Based on dividends of $2.08 per share for the trailing twelve months and the last price of $71.76 the dividend yield is 2.9%.

December 31 2025: Alliant Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

This MDA includes information relating to Alliant Energy, IPL and WPL (collectively, the Utilities), as well as ATC Holdings, AEF and Corporate Services. Where appropriate, information relating to a specific entity has been segregated and labeled as such. The following discussion and analysis should be read in conjunction with the Financial Statements and Notes included in this report. Unless otherwise noted, all "per share" references in MDA refer to earnings per diluted share. In addition, this MDA includes certain financial information for 2025 compared to 2024. Refer to MDA in the combined 2024 Form 10-K for details on certain financial information for 2024 compared to 2023.

OVERVIEW

Mission, Purpose and Strategy

Alliant Energy's mission is to deliver the energy solutions and exceptional service that its customers and communities count on - affordably, safely, reliably and responsibly. This mission aligns with Alliant Energy's purpose - to serve customers and build stronger communities - which guides it through the evolving dynamics of the economy and the energy industry. Alliant Energy leads as a corporate citizen, advancing environmental stewardship and supporting the communities in its service territories. Alliant Energy's mission and purpose are supported by a strategy focused on meeting evolving customer expectations, delivering attractive returns for investors, and advancing emerging technologies and generation to enable safe, secure and future-ready energy production. This strategy includes the following key elements:

Providing affordable energy solutions for customers - Alliant Energy's strategy focuses on affordable energy solutions that support retention and growth of existing customers and attract new customers to its service territories.

Key Highlights -

Alliant Energy's resource plan is the roadmap for building a strong and resilient energy future to meet the growing energy needs across Iowa and Wisconsin.

November 06 2025: Alliant Energy Announces Third Quarter 2025 Results and 2026 Guidance

- Narrowed 2025 ongoing earnings guidance range to $3.17 - $3.23 per share enabled by solid results through the third quarter

- Announced 2026 earnings guidance range of $3.36 - $3.46 per share (6.6% increase over 2025) and 2026 annual common stock dividend target of $2.14 per share (5.4% increase over 2025)

- Increased contracted data center demand to 3 GW, representing a 50% increase in peak load demand by 2030

- Increased 4-year capital expenditure forecast by 17% to $13.4 billion to meet growing demand

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) today announced U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) consolidated unaudited earnings per share (EPS) of $1.09 for the third quarter of 2025, compared to $1.15 for the same quarter in 2024. The ongoing EPS for the third quarter was $1.12, compared to $1.15 in the same quarter in 2024.

For the first nine months of 2025, Alliant Energy reported EPS of $2.59, a 23.3% increase from $2.10 for the same period in 2024. The ongoing EPS for the first nine months of 2025 were $2.62, a 12.4% increase from $2.33 in the same period in 2024. Based on results for the first nine months of 2025, ongoing earnings per share guidance was narrowed to $3.17 to $3.23, with full year 2025 earnings trending toward the upper-half of this range.

Alliant Energy's utilities Interstate Power and Light Company (IPL) and Wisconsin Power and Light Company (WPL) now have 3 gigawatts of contracted demand from data centers with the inclusion of the recently executed electric service agreement (ESA) for 900 megawatts for the QTS Madison site. With the signed agreement Alliant Energy expects its peak energy demand to grow an industry leading 50% by 2030. The energy resources to serve this expected load, while maintaining safe and reliable service to all customers, have increased the Company's forecasted capital expenditures for 2026-2029 to $13.4 billion.

The Company announced 2026 earnings guidance at $3.36 to $3.46 per share, continuing its strong 10-year track record of compound annual earnings growth of 6%.

2025 HIGHLIGHTS

Key highlights since the filing of the 2024 Form 10-K include the following:

Customer Investments:

Over the next six years, Alliant Energy currently plans to develop and/or acquire new generation investments to add flexibility with evolving load growth, including approximately 2,000 MW of natural gas resources, approximately 1,300 MW of new energy storage, approximately 1,100 MW of new renewable generation, improvements of approximately 530 MW at existing natural gas-fired EGUs, and refurbishments at approximately 450 MW of existing wind farms. Alliant Energy is currently evaluating the impact of potential additional large load growth customers and MISO's seasonal resource adequacy requirements on its resource plans and will update these generation investment plans as needed in the future. Estimated capital expenditures for these planned projects for 2025 through 2029 are included in the "Generation" section in the construction and acquisition table in "Liquidity and Capital Resources." In February 2025, WPL filed a certificate of authority (CA) application with the PSCW for approval to construct a 2 billion cubic feet, or 25 million gallon, liquified natural gas facility in Rock County, Wisconsin.

May 07 2026: Jefferies Adjusts Alliant Energy Price Target to $85, Signaling Confidence

Jefferies has raised its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation to $85 from $84, reflecting a sustained confidence in the company's growth trajectory. This adjustment, made on 8th May 2026, comes as the firm evaluates the utility's performance amid favorable market conditions and robust operational fundamentals.

The updated price target is backed by a composite rating system that considers several key metrics, including valuation, earnings per share (EPS) revisions over four months, and overall visibility. Alliant Energy's ratings indicate a solid standing in its sector, which is essential for attracting investor interest in a competitive market.

Investors may find reassurance in Alliant Energy's strong composite ratings, which aggregate various aspects of the company's financial health and market performance. Jefferies' analysis suggests that Alliant is well-positioned for future growth, benefiting from sound fundamentals and profitability metrics that surpass many of its peers.

Furthermore, Alliant Energy's commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices plays a crucial role in its appeal. The company's MSCI ESG score reflects its dedication to sustainability, allowing investors to align their portfolios with socially responsible investments. This focus on ESG factors is increasingly relevant in today's investment landscape, where environmental considerations are becoming a priority for many investors.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy sector, the revised $85 price target from Jefferies serves as a reminder of the company's resilience and potential for long-term success. Investors will be keen to monitor Alliant's performance in the coming quarters, particularly in light of its robust ratings and the broader market dynamics.

SECTION 4 CORPORATE PROFILE

4.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

ALLIANT ENERGY CORPORATION EX-DIVIDENDS SINCE APR 30

The shares decreased by 2.3% in the 6 trading sessions since the ex-dividend date of April 30. The company paid a dividend of 53.50c per share. Based on dividends of $2.08 per share for the trailing twelve months and the last price of $71.76 the dividend yield is 2.9%.

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 23, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Alliant Energy Net Profit up 17%

Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $810m, up 17% from $690m in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 17% from $2.69 in FY2024 to $3.15 in FY2025.

SECTION 4 CORPORATE PROFILE

4.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

ALLIANT ENERGY CORPORATION EX-DIVIDENDS SINCE APR 30

The shares decreased by 2.3% in the 6 trading sessions since the ex-dividend date of April 30. The company paid a dividend of 53.50c per share. Based on dividends of $2.08 per share for the trailing twelve months and the last price of $71.76 the dividend yield is 2.9%.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 23, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Alliant Energy Net Profit up 17%

Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $810m, up 17% from $690m in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 17% from $2.69 in FY2024 to $3.15 in FY2025.

MADISON, Friday, May 08 2026 - Alliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ: LNT $71.76) stock price increased 0.1% but fell short of the 12-day Exponential Moving Average [EMA] of $72.09. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 671.0 points (2.4%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.3%. The stock last traded at $71.76. There were 2,274,239 shares worth $163.2 million traded today; the volume was 1.1 times the average daily trading volume of 2.1 million shares.

December 31 2025: Alliant Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

This MDA includes information relating to Alliant Energy, IPL and WPL (collectively, the Utilities), as well as ATC Holdings, AEF and Corporate Services. Where appropriate, information relating to a specific entity has been segregated and labeled as such. The following discussion and analysis should be read in conjunction with the Financial Statements and Notes included in this report. Unless otherwise noted, all "per share" references in MDA refer to earnings per diluted share. In addition, this MDA includes certain financial information for 2025 compared to 2024. Refer to MDA in the combined 2024 Form 10-K for details on certain financial information for 2024 compared to 2023.

OVERVIEW

Mission, Purpose and Strategy

Alliant Energy's mission is to deliver the energy solutions and exceptional service that its customers and communities count on - affordably, safely, reliably and responsibly. This mission aligns with Alliant Energy's purpose - to serve customers and build stronger communities - which guides it through the evolving dynamics of the economy and the energy industry. Alliant Energy leads as a corporate citizen, advancing environmental stewardship and supporting the communities in its service territories. Alliant Energy's mission and purpose are supported by a strategy focused on meeting evolving customer expectations, delivering attractive returns for investors, and advancing emerging technologies and generation to enable safe, secure and future-ready energy production. This strategy includes the following key elements:

Providing affordable energy solutions for customers - Alliant Energy's strategy focuses on affordable energy solutions that support retention and growth of existing customers and attract new customers to its service territories.

Key Highlights -

Alliant Energy's resource plan is the roadmap for building a strong and resilient energy future to meet the growing energy needs across Iowa and Wisconsin.

Press Release article 4 of 13, Source: Company Website, 304 words

December 02 2025: Wisconsin Power and Light Company Prices Public Offering of Debentures

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Wisconsin Power and Light Company ("WPL"), a wholly owned subsidiary of Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT), announced the pricing of its public offering of $300 million aggregate principal amount of 5.700% debentures due 2055. The debentures will be due on December 15, 2055. WPL intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to reduce its outstanding commercial paper and for general corporate purposes. The closing of the offering is expected to occur on December 5, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The offering was marketed through a group of underwriters consisting of Mizuho Securities USA LLC, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, BofA Securities, Inc. and MUFG Securities Americas Inc., as joint book-running managers, and Academy Securities, Inc., PNC Capital Markets LLC and U.S. Bancorp Investments, Inc., as co-managers.

The offering is being made only by means of a prospectus supplement and accompanying prospectus which are part of a shelf registration statement WPL filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission"). Copies may be obtained from Mizuho Securities USA LLC by calling toll free at 1-866-271-7403, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC by calling toll free at 1-800-645-3751, BofA Securities, Inc. by calling toll free at 1-800-294-1322, and MUFG Securities Americas Inc. by calling toll-free at 1-877-649-6848. Electronic copies of these documents will be available from the Commission's website at www.sec.gov.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy these securities, nor will there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

Press Release article 5 of 13, Source: Company Website, 357 words

November 06 2025: Alliant Energy Announces Third Quarter 2025 Results and 2026 Guidance

- Narrowed 2025 ongoing earnings guidance range to $3.17 - $3.23 per share enabled by solid results through the third quarter

- Announced 2026 earnings guidance range of $3.36 - $3.46 per share (6.6% increase over 2025) and 2026 annual common stock dividend target of $2.14 per share (5.4% increase over 2025)

- Increased contracted data center demand to 3 GW, representing a 50% increase in peak load demand by 2030

- Increased 4-year capital expenditure forecast by 17% to $13.4 billion to meet growing demand

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) today announced U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) consolidated unaudited earnings per share (EPS) of $1.09 for the third quarter of 2025, compared to $1.15 for the same quarter in 2024. The ongoing EPS for the third quarter was $1.12, compared to $1.15 in the same quarter in 2024.

For the first nine months of 2025, Alliant Energy reported EPS of $2.59, a 23.3% increase from $2.10 for the same period in 2024. The ongoing EPS for the first nine months of 2025 were $2.62, a 12.4% increase from $2.33 in the same period in 2024. Based on results for the first nine months of 2025, ongoing earnings per share guidance was narrowed to $3.17 to $3.23, with full year 2025 earnings trending toward the upper-half of this range.

Alliant Energy's utilities Interstate Power and Light Company (IPL) and Wisconsin Power and Light Company (WPL) now have 3 gigawatts of contracted demand from data centers with the inclusion of the recently executed electric service agreement (ESA) for 900 megawatts for the QTS Madison site. With the signed agreement Alliant Energy expects its peak energy demand to grow an industry leading 50% by 2030. The energy resources to serve this expected load, while maintaining safe and reliable service to all customers, have increased the Company's forecasted capital expenditures for 2026-2029 to $13.4 billion.

The Company announced 2026 earnings guidance at $3.36 to $3.46 per share, continuing its strong 10-year track record of compound annual earnings growth of 6%.

Press Release article 7 of 13, Source: Company Website, 208 words

October 07 2025: Events Calendar: Alliant Energy Corporation Announces Third Quarter Earnings Release and Conference Call

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) has scheduled its third quarter earnings release for Thursday, November 6th, after market close. A conference call to review the third quarter results is scheduled for Friday, November 7th at 9 a.m. CT.

Alliant Energy will webcast the event live at ww.alliantenergy.com/investors or https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/288503171. The call is open to the public and will be hosted by Lisa Barton, President and CEO; and Robert Durian, Executive Vice President and CFO.

An archive of the webcast will be available on the company's website at www.alliantenergy.com/investors.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) provides regulated energy service to approximately 1 million electric and 430,000 natural gas customers across Iowa and Wisconsin. Alliant Energy's mission is to deliver energy solutions and exceptional service customers and communities count on - safely, efficiently and responsibly. Interstate Power and Light Company (IPL) and Wisconsin Power and Light Company (WPL) are Alliant Energy's two public energy companies. Alliant Energy is a component of Bloomberg's Gender-Equality Index and the S&P 500. For more information, visit alliantenergy.com and follow Alliant Energy on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram and X.

September 30 2025: Alliant Energy: File SEC Form 10-Q - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-Q

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

This MDA includes information relating to Alliant Energy, and IPL and WPL (collectively, the Utilities), as well as ATC Holdings, AEF and Corporate Services. Where appropriate, information relating to a specific entity has been segregated and labeled as such. The following discussion and analysis should be read in conjunction with the Financial Statements and the Notes included in this report, as well as the financial statements, notes and MDA included in the 2024 Form 10-K. Unless otherwise noted, all "per share" references in MDA refer to earnings per diluted share.

2025 HIGHLIGHTS

Key highlights since the filing of the 2024 Form 10-K include the following:

Customer Investments:

Over the next six years, Alliant Energy currently plans to develop and/or acquire new generation investments to add flexibility with evolving load growth, including approximately 2,000 MW of natural gas resources, approximately 1,300 MW of new energy storage, approximately 1,100 MW of new renewable generation, improvements of approximately 530 MW at existing natural gas-fired EGUs, and refurbishments at approximately 450 MW of existing wind farms. Alliant Energy is currently evaluating the impact of potential additional large load growth customers and MISO's seasonal resource adequacy requirements on its resource plans and will update these generation investment plans as needed in the future. Estimated capital expenditures for these planned projects for 2025 through 2029 are included in the "Generation" section in the construction and acquisition table in "Liquidity and Capital Resources." Information on IPL's and WPL's regulatory filings and/or approvals for future generation and energy storage projects are as follows:

In February 2025, WPL filed a certificate of authority (CA) application with the PSCW for approval to construct a 2 billion cubic feet, or 25 million gallon, liquified natural gas facility in Rock County, Wisconsin.

September 23 2025: Alliant Energy Prices Offering of $725 Million of 5.750% Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation ("Alliant Energy") (NASDAQ: LNT) announced the pricing of its public offering of $725 million aggregate principal amount of 5.750% junior subordinated notes due 2056. The junior subordinated notes will be due on April 1, 2056. Alliant Energy intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to reduce outstanding commercial paper, retire long term debt and/or for general corporate purposes. July 23 2025: Alliant Energy releases new Corporate Responsibility Report

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) published its latest Corporate Responsibility Report (CRR) today. The report includes updates on the company's sustainability data for 2024. The report also highlights the company's balanced mix of energy sources, employee engagement opportunities and positive impact on the communities it serves.

"At Alliant Energy, we are growing and will continue to grow at the pace of our customers and communities," said Lisa Barton, President and CEO, Alliant Energy. "By powering opportunity, we're helping build stronger, more resilient communities for generations to come. From data center development to local investments in smart energy solutions, every step we take is grounded in trust, transparency and measurable value."

The report also demonstrates the company's commitment to make things better. In 2024, Alliant Energy, its employees and retirees, and the company's charitable foundation collectively contributed more than $9 million and volunteered over 80,000 hours.

Source: Company Website

Press Release article 12 of 13, Source: WEC, 78 words

July 22 2025: Alliant Energy quarterly dividends steady

Alliant Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 50.75c per share for Q2/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q1/2025) dividend of 50.75c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, July 31, 2025 and the record date is Thursday, July 31, 2025 and it is payable on Friday, August 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended July 22 were $1.97.

July 08 2025: Events Calendar: Alliant Energy Corporation Announces Second Quarter Earnings Release and Conference Call

MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) has scheduled its second quarter earnings release for Thursday, August 7th, after market close. A conference call to review the second quarter results is scheduled for Friday, August 8th at 9 a.m. CT.

Alliant Energy will webcast the event live at www.alliantenergy.com/investors. The call is open to the public and will be hosted by Lisa Barton, President and CEO; and Robert Durian, Executive Vice President and CFO. Individuals who would like to participate in the conference call can do so by dialing (800) 549-8228 (Toll Free - North America) or (646) 564-2877 (International). The conference ID is 78071.

An archive of the webcast will be available on the company's website at www.alliantenergy.com/investors.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) provides regulated energy service to approximately 1 million electric and 430,000 natural gas customers across Iowa and Wisconsin. Alliant Energy's mission is to deliver energy solutions and exceptional service customers and communities count on - safely, efficiently and responsibly. Interstate Power and Light Company (IPL) and Wisconsin Power and Light Company (WPL) are Alliant Energy's two public energy companies. Alliant Energy is a component of Bloomberg's Gender-Equality Index and the S&P 500. For more information, visit alliantenergy.com and follow Alliant Energy on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram and X.

Source: Company Website

May 07 2026: Jefferies Adjusts Alliant Energy Price Target to $85, Signaling Confidence

Jefferies has raised its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation to $85 from $84, reflecting a sustained confidence in the company's growth trajectory. This adjustment, made on 8th May 2026, comes as the firm evaluates the utility's performance amid favorable market conditions and robust operational fundamentals.

The updated price target is backed by a composite rating system that considers several key metrics, including valuation, earnings per share (EPS) revisions over four months, and overall visibility. Alliant Energy's ratings indicate a solid standing in its sector, which is essential for attracting investor interest in a competitive market.

Investors may find reassurance in Alliant Energy's strong composite ratings, which aggregate various aspects of the company's financial health and market performance. Jefferies' analysis suggests that Alliant is well-positioned for future growth, benefiting from sound fundamentals and profitability metrics that surpass many of its peers.

Furthermore, Alliant Energy's commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices plays a crucial role in its appeal. The company's MSCI ESG score reflects its dedication to sustainability, allowing investors to align their portfolios with socially responsible investments. This focus on ESG factors is increasingly relevant in today's investment landscape, where environmental considerations are becoming a priority for many investors.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy sector, the revised $85 price target from Jefferies serves as a reminder of the company's resilience and potential for long-term success. Investors will be keen to monitor Alliant's performance in the coming quarters, particularly in light of its robust ratings and the broader market dynamics.

SECTION 4 CORPORATE PROFILE

4.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

value.Title: The Rewind: May 1

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-M713-S0TV-6193-00000-00The Rewind: May 12026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisREWINDThe Rewind: May 1Staff Writer

In case you missed it: Forest City wildlife tracking tower dedicated to young birder

The Winnebago County Conservation Board dedicated a Motus wildlife tracking tower at Hanson Nature Center in honor of Mae Helgeson, an 11-year-old birder who died in an accident. The tower, part of the international Motus Wildlife Tracking System, monitors birds, bats, and butterflies in north-central Iowa, contributing to global migration research.

The Helgeson family funded the tower through memorial donations, transforming their grief into a public resource. The tower offers educational opportunities with interactive displays and Mae's photography. It fills a geographic gap in the tracking network, enhancing understanding of migratory species' routes and behaviors.

Read full article

Alaskan adventure: Osage teacher goes north for the Iditarod dog sled race

Kari Wright, a third-grade teacher at Lincoln Elementary in Osage, experienced the Iditarod as the 2026 Teacher on the Trail in March. She flew in a bush plane, met mushers, and blogged about the race, connecting it to classroom lessons. Wright has used the Iditarod for educational activities for 19 years, inspiring students in subjects like math and science.

Despite falling ill and missing part of the race, Wright recovered to witness the finish in Nome. Her students engaged with her journey via FaceTime, enhancing their learning experience. Former students expressed excitement, recalling their past Iditarod activities.

Read full article

Cerro Gordo County officials clash with state over solar development

The Iowa Utilities Commission advanced the review of River City Energy LLC's proposed 500-megawatt solar project in Cerro Gordo County, despite local opposition. The commission's April 27 order requires more information from the company, while the county has enacted a ban on such developments after a temporary moratorium.

The state commission's decision highlights the tension between state and local authorities over energy infrastructure. While Iowa law supports renewable energy growth, Cerro Gordo County officials argue these projects conflict with local land-use priorities. The commission has yet to decide on the generating certificate, with potential impacts on future renewable projects in rural areas.

Read full article

In case you missed it: Veteran bull rider from Brazil competes in Mason City event

Pedro Fonesca, a 32-year-old Brazilian native now living in Elk River, Minnesota, balances running a construction business with bull riding. A U.S. citizen for a year, Fonesca won the 2025 Rice Rodeo Tour bull riding championship and recently competed in Mason City. He began riding bulls at 15 in Brazil and moved to the U.S. at 25 to compete in professional events.

Fonesca, who stays close to home for competitions, appreciates the adrenaline and personal challenge of bull riding. His brother Joao also rides bulls in the U.S. despite past injuries. The Rice Rodeo Tour will visit Worth and Mitchell County fairs this summer.

Read full article

In case you missed it: North Iowa historical museum is being refreshed with some new exhibits

Mary Loden, appointed director of the Kinney Pioneer Museum in June 2025, is spearheading efforts to revitalize the museum near Mason City. Loden aims to enhance visibility from Highway 122 through landscaping and reduce interior clutter. She is developing new exhibits, including one on John Dillinger's 1934 bank robbery and another on John Kopecky, founder of the North Iowa Band Festival.

The museum will host several events in 2026, starting with a vintage tractor ride on May 9-10. Other events include a car show on July 18 and the Apple Cider and Donut Day on Sept. 27. Loden seeks more volunteers and board members to support the museum's efforts.

Read full article

value.Title: Xcel gas plant to help replace Sherco coal, back up renewables

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-S2S3-RW4R-4086-00000-00Xcel gas plant to help replace Sherco coal, back up renewables2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:13ZLexisNexisMONTICELLO TIMESXcel gas plant to help replace Sherco coal, back up renewablesLauren Flaum Monticello Times

A new natural gas plant planned in southwest Minnesota will help support the transition away from coal at Xcel Energy's Sherco facility in Becker, as the utility continues reshaping how it delivers electricity across the region.

The Minnesota Public Utilities Commission recently approved Xcel Energy's plan to build the 420-megawatt Lyon County Generating Station near Garvin. Construction is expected to begin later this summer, with the plant slated to come online in 2028.

While the facility itself will be located far from central Minnesota, its output will connect into the broader grid through the Minnesota Energy Connection transmission line a major project that links new wind and solar generation in southwest Minnesota to the Sherco Energy Hub in Becker.

Xcel officials said the plant will play a key role in maintaining reliability as the company retires its remaining coal-fired units, including those at Sherco, by 2030.

Natural gas peaking plants operate differently than traditional power plants. Rather than running continuously, they are used during periods of high demand or when renewable energy sources such as wind and solar are not producing.

According to Xcel, the Lyon County plant is expected to operate between 5% and 30% of the year, depending on weather conditions and how much carbon-free energy is available on the grid.

That stands in contrast to Xcel's nuclear facilities including the Monticello plant which operate around the clock and typically shut down only for maintenance and refueling.

"Natural gas peaking plants like the Lyon County Generating Station provide as-needed support to those renewables to ensure we keep the grid reliable for our customers," said Kevin Coss, a principal media relations representative for Xcel Energy.

Electricity generated at the new facility will travel along the Minnesota Energy Connection line and tie into existing interconnection points at the Sherco site, helping replace energy once produced by coal units there.

In addition to the gas plant, the transmission project is expected to carry up to 4,000 megawatts of new wind and solar energy enough to power more than 1 million homes across the Upper Midwest.

Xcel said the combination of renewable energy, natural gas peaking plants and always-available sources such as nuclear power will be critical to maintaining grid stability as the state moves toward carbon-free electricity goals.

The company has not announced how long natural gas facilities like the Lyon County plant will remain part of its long-term energy mix, but officials say they are needed to balance the variability of renewable generation in the near term.

Overall, Xcel said the new plant will support reliability not only in southwest Minnesota, but across centralMinnesota and the broader Upper Midwest grid.

value.Title: Pawlenty to lead solar industry group Former GOP governor supported clean energy initiatives during his two terms
value.Overview: St. Paul Pioneer Press (Minnesota), NEWS; Pg. A2, 349words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-3CP3-SCNX-34W2-00000-00Pawlenty to lead solar industry group2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:13ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A2Pawlenty to lead solar industry groupFormer GOP governor supported clean energy initiatives during his two termsBy Alex DerosierADEROSIER@PIONEERPRESS.COM

Former Minnesota Gov. Tim Pawlenty will serve as the next president and CEO of the Solar Energy Industries Association.

Pawlenty, a Republican who held the state ’s top office from 2003 to 2011, backed a number of renewable and clean energy initiatives as governor. He’ll start his new role with the solar group on June 15, according to a news release.

“Tim brings a proven track record of executive leadership, deep public policy expertise, and a clear understanding of the energy challenges facing our country,” association board chair Scott Moskowitz said in the news release.

The Solar Energy Industries Association represents about 1,200 member companies from the solar and energy storage industry. One of its initiatives was promoting the “ambitious” target of 30% solar electricity generation in the U.S. by the next decade.

“At a time when affordability is front and center for Americans, expanding access to low-cost solar and storage can deliver real savings and strengthen our domestic energy supply,” the former governor said in the release. “I look forward to working with SEIA’s members, policymakers, and partners to move that vision forward.”

Pawlenty stood out as a Republican governor for his strong support for clean energy initiatives, including signing a bill requiring the state to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 80% between 2005 and 2050 and to get 25% of its energy from renewable sources by 2025.

As of 2024, 33% of Minnesota’s in-state power generation was from renewables, according to the U.S. Department of Energy.

In addition to the Next Generation Energy Act, Pawlenty backed tax credits and other incentives to attract green jobs to Minnesota.

Pawlenty, 65, ran for president in 2012 before dropping out of the race and serving as national co-chair of Mitt Romney’s presidential campaign. He left that job before election day to lead the Financial Services Roundtable, a banking industry group.

He held that job until 2018, when he attempted to run for a third term as governor but lost in the Republican primary to then-Hennepin County Commissioner Jeff Johnson.

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT $71.76), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $2.30 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $71.76. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 1,524.6 points (or 5.5%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $590.2 million to $18.4 billion.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

ALLIANT ENERGY CORPORATION EX-DIVIDENDS SINCE APR 30

The shares decreased by 2.3% in the 6 trading sessions since the ex-dividend date of April 30. The company paid a dividend of 53.50c per share. Based on dividends of $2.08 per share for the trailing twelve months and the last price of $71.76 the dividend yield is 2.9%.

1.4 STOCK SNIPPETS - ESG

July 20 2022: Alliant Energys 2022 Corporate Responsibility Report highlights the companys Climate Report and biodiversity commitment

Company focuses on serving customers and building stronger communities through environmental, social and governance commitments

MADISON, Wis., July 20, 2022 -- Today, Alliant Energy releases its 2022 Corporate Responsibility Report (CRR), which includes the companys Climate Report and outlines its commitment to biodiversity. The CRR also highlights the companys Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) programs, which connect to Alliant Energys purpose to serve customers and build stronger communities.

Responsibility to our customers and the communities we serve is at the heart of everything we do, said John Larsen, Alliant Energy Chair, President and CEO. "That responsibility extends well beyond energy and guides our approach to all our environmental, social and governance commitments. It also comes with the strong understanding that the decisions we make impact the world around us.

Alliant Energys 2022 Corporate Responsibility Report highlights

Environmental: Alliant Energys Climate Report provides data connecting the companys Clean Energy Blueprint and carbon dioxide reduction goals to be consistent with the United Nations Paris Agreement objective to limit global average temperature rise. The report is based on the results from a study completed by the Electric Power Research Institute. Alliant Energy will use the findings as a guide while they continue work toward their aspirational goal of net-zero carbon dioxide emissions from the electricity the company generates by 2050.

The Corporate Responsibility Report also includes the companys Biodiversity Commitment, which solidifies their longstanding tradition of caring for the environment. Alliant Energys biodiversity efforts include protecting natural resources and wildlife, especially those that are threatened or endangered.

The One Million Trees initiative Alliant Energy announced last July is a component of the Biodiversity Commitment.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

urn:contentItem:6JHT-Y9P3-S8SM-536V-00000-00Xcel Energy to spend $12 Million to occupy 37,400 square feet of space in Amarillo Texas.2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:43:47ZLexisNexisXcel Energy to spend $12 Million to occupy 37,400 square feet of space in Amarillo Texas.Craig Etkin

Amarillo, Texas - According to state and local development sources, Xcel Energy plans to invest $12 Million to build out 37,400 square feet of new space in Amarillo. The company plans to occupy the new space at 4701 Parkside Drive in Amarillo, on or about July 1, 2027. According to the company website, As a leading electric and natural gas energy company, we offer a comprehensive portfolio of energy-related products and services to 3.4 million electricity customers and 1.9 million natural gas customers across our eight states: Colorado, Michigan, Minnesota, New Mexico, North Dakota, South Dakota, Texas and Wisconsin. Our workforce of more than 12,000 is rising to the challenge of a dynamic, constantly-changing utility industry. One that requires us to be even more customer focused, forward thinking and productive while remaining committed to meeting our customers fundamental need for safe, reliable, affordable energy. Do you have a passion for renewable energy, like wind or solar? Or care about giving back to the community? Youll be in a position to directly impact our energy future. At Xcel Energy, youll be challenged, respected and rewarded. Youll find an ethical team committed to excellence, safety and environmental stewardship. A dynamic company where youll have meaningful work that makes a difference.

To learn more about Xcel Energy, visit http://www.xcelenergy.com/

Company Contact:
Xcel Energy
401 Nicollet Mall
Minneapolis, MN55401
Phone: 612-330-5500
LinkedIn Link: https://www.linkedin.com/company/xcel-energy/
